verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B encodes the beta subunit of the mitochondrial ATP synthase F1 complex (F1-ATPase), canonically located in the mitochondrial inner membrane facing the matrix. However, a well-documented cell-surface ecto-ATP synthase pool has been described on multiple tumor cell types (hepatocellular carcinoma, lung cancer, endothelial cells, colon cancer) and on some normal cell types (liver sinusoidal endothelial cells, adipocytes). On the outer leaflet, it functions as an angiostatin receptor and is targeted by cancer-directed antibodies. This surface pool is distinct from the canonical mitochondrial location and is considered cell-state/tumor-context-induced or present as a minority surface pool in specialized somatic lineages. The ecto-form lacks a TM domain of its own and the anchoring mechanism is not fully resolved, but surface biotinylation and flow cytometry confirm outer-leaflet localization. Fits cell_state_induced (cancer ecto-display) contextual category.
predicted_key_uncertainty: The mechanism anchoring ecto-ATP5F1B to the outer leaflet is not fully characterized; no TM domain or GPI anchor is known, so the wash-resistance and precise tethering partner remain uncertain.
